questions.question: What is a numeric enum in TypeScript?
options: A) An enum where members are assigned string values
B) An enum where members are auto-assigned incrementing numeric values starting from 0 by default
C) An enum that can only hold even numbers
D) A type alias for number

questions.question:
What is the value of `Direction.South`?

```ts
enum Direction {
  North,
  South,
  East,
  West
}
```
options: C) 'South'

questions.question: What is a string enum?
options: A) An enum that auto-generates string values
B) An enum where each member is explicitly assigned a string value
C) A type alias for a set of strings
D) An enum that converts numbers to strings

questions.question:
What is the value of `Color.Green`?

```ts
enum Color {
  Red = 'RED',
  Green = 'GREEN',
  Blue = 'BLUE'
}
```
options: B) 'Green'
C) 'GREEN'

questions.question: What does `const enum` do differently from a regular enum?
options: A) It prevents the enum from being used
B) It is completely inlined at compile time, producing no runtime JavaScript object
C) It makes all values constant numbers
D) It is identical to a regular enum

questions.question: What does `Partial<T>` do?
options: A) Makes all properties of T required
B) Makes all properties of T optional
C) Removes all properties from T
D) Makes all properties readonly

questions.question: What is the result type of `Partial<{ name: string; age: number }>`?
options: A) { name: string; age: number }
B) { name?: string; age?: number }
C) { name: string | undefined; age: number | undefined }

questions.question: What does `Required<T>` do?
options: A) Makes all properties optional
B) Makes all properties of T required (removes the ? modifier)
C) Adds a 'required' field to T
D) Makes the type immutable

questions.question: What does `Pick<T, K>` do?
options: A) Removes the specified keys from T
B) Creates a type with only the specified keys K from T
C) Picks a random property from T
D) Validates that K exists in T

questions.question: What is the result of `Pick<{ name: string; age: number; email: string }, 'name' | 'email'>`?
options: A) { age: number }
B) { name: string; email: string }
C) { name: string; age: number; email: string }
D) string

questions.question: What does `Omit<T, K>` do?
options: A) Keeps only the specified keys
B) Creates a type with all properties of T except the specified keys K
C) Makes the specified keys optional
D) Makes the specified keys readonly

questions.question: What is the result of `Omit<{ name: string; age: number; email: string }, 'age'>`?
options: A) { age: number }
B) { name: string; email: string }
C) { name: string; age: number; email: string }
D) never

questions.question: What does `Record<K, V>` do?
options: A) Creates an array of key-value pairs
B) Creates an object type with keys of type K and values of type V
C) Records changes to an object
D) Creates a map data structure
A) { math: number; english: number }
B) An object where any string key maps to a number value
C) Map<string, number>
D) [string, number][]

questions.question: What does `Readonly<T>` do?
options: A) Makes all properties of T writable
B) Makes all properties of T readonly
C) Freezes the object at runtime
D) Makes the type immutable only in strict mode
A) No error
B) Compile error: Cannot assign to 'name' because it is a read-only property
C) Runtime error
D) Warning only

questions.question: What does `ReturnType<T>` extract?
options: A) The parameter types of function T
B) The return type of function type T
C) The class type of T
D) The name of the function
A) typeof greet
B) (name: string) => string
C) string
D) void

questions.question: What does `Parameters<T>` extract?
options: A) The return type of function T
B) A tuple type of the parameter types of function type T
C) The number of parameters
D) The names of the parameters

questions.question: What is the result of `Parameters<typeof greet>` given `function greet(name: string, age: number): void`?
options: A) [string, number]
B) { name: string; age: number }
C) string | number
D) void
A) It prints 0
B) It prints 'Active' — numeric enums support both name-to-value and value-to-name lookups
C) It throws an error
D) It prints undefined

questions.question: Do string enums support reverse mapping?
options: A) Yes, just like numeric enums
B) No, string enums do NOT generate reverse mappings
C) Only with const enums
D) Only when using the --reverseMap flag

questions.question: What is the difference between `Pick` and `Omit`?
options: A) They are identical
B) Pick selects specified keys; Omit removes specified keys
C) Pick works on arrays; Omit works on objects
D) Pick is for types; Omit is for interfaces
A) Required<User>
B) Readonly<User>
C) Partial<User>
D) Pick<User, 'name'>
A) 'Banana'
B) 'BANANA'
D) Compile error
